workers.result_summary:
I have now read the complete content of all files across all 5 directories. Here is the structured report:

### SUMMARY
Successfully read the complete content of all 43 Dart source files across 5 feature directories (diagnostics, dashboard, config, report, settings) in the NetDiagnostic cross-platform Flutter application. The codebase follows a clean architecture with domain/engine/presentation layers, featuring 5 diagnostic groups (G1-G5) with platform-specific implementations (Windows/Linux/macOS/Android/iOS) plus a mock simulator, a Flutter Riverpod-based presentation layer with provider state management, and feature modules for dashboard, config, report, and settings screens.

### EVIDENCE
- `/lib/features/diagnostics/domain/entities/diagnostic_result.dart:1-113` — Immutable model for test outcomes
- `/lib/features/diagnostics/domain/entities/result_property.dart:1-29` — Key-value property with severity enum
- `/lib/features/diagnostics/domain/enums/test_status.dart:1-9` — `TestStatus` enum (pass/fail/warning/error/skipped/info)
- `/lib/features/diagnostics/engine/g1_base.dart:1-50` — Abstract G1 base with 7 test method signatures
- `/lib/features/diagnostics/engine/g1_windows.dart:1-399` — Windows G1: PowerShell-based network adapter/DHCP/IP/netstat tests
- `/lib/features/diagnostics/engine/g1_linux.dart:1-445` — Linux G1: ip/ifconfig/iw/nmcli/lspci-based adapter tests
- `/lib/features/diagnostics/engine/g1_test.dart:1-71` — Standalone Windows G1 test runner
- `/lib/features/diagnostics/engine/g1_linux_test.dart:1-65` — Standalone Linux G1 test runner
- `/lib/features/diagnostics/engine/g2_base.dart:1-21` — Abstract G2 base (connectivity/security: network profile, TCP, gateway, routing, ARP, proxy)
- `/lib/features/diagnostics/engine/g2_windows.dart:1-122` — Windows G2: PowerShell/route/netsh implementation
- `/lib/features/diagnostics/engine/g2_linux.dart:1-81` — Linux G2: nmcli/sysctl/ip/neigh/env implementation
- `/lib/features/diagnostics/engine/g2_linux_test.dart:1-54` — Standalone Linux G2 test runner
- `/lib/features/diagnostics/engine/g3_base.dart:1-21` — Abstract G3 base (Internet/DNS: Netskope, DNS servers/cache/pollution, connectivity, speed)
- `/lib/features/diagnostics/engine/g3_windows.dart:1-178` — Windows G3: PowerShell/tasklist/ping/HttpClient implementation
- `/lib/features/diagnostics/engine/g3_linux.dart:1-148` — Linux G3: pgrep/resolvectl/dig/ping/HttpClient implementation
- `/lib/features/diagnostics/engine/g3_linux_test.dart:1-55` — Standalone Linux G3 test runner
- `/lib/features/diagnostics/engine/g4_base.dart:1-24` — Abstract G4 base (remote host: DNS resolution, ping, traceroute, pathPing, MTU, port scan)
- `/lib/features/diagnostics/engine/g4_adapter.dart:1-167` — G4 adapter: delegates to g4_remote_host with async port scanner (20 concurrent workers)
- `/lib/features/diagnostics/engine/g4_remote_host.dart:1-1256` — Full G4 remote host engine (1256 lines): platform-aware DNS/ping/traceroute/pathPing/MTU with locale-independent output parsing
- `/lib/features/diagnostics/engine/g5_base.dart:1-34` — Abstract G5 base (website/URL: URL parsing, TCP connect, service banner, curl, HTTP headers, security/SSL, redirect, compression, timing, FTP, SSH, email)
- `/lib/features/diagnostics/engine/g5_adapter.dart:1-95` — G5 adapter: delegates all 12 tests to g5_website_url
- `/lib/features/diagnostics/engine/g5_website_url.dart:1-1511` — Full G5 website engine (1511 lines): HTTP/dart-based URL validation, TCP connect, banner grab, curl verbose, HTTP headers, security headers, SSL cert, redirect chain, compression, timing, FTP/SSH/email diagnostics
- `/lib/features/diagnostics/engine/diagnostic_engine.dart:1-262` — Orchestrator: platform factories, native plugin dispatch, per-group routing with timeout/skip handling
- `/lib/features/diagnostics/engine/runner/platform_command.dart:1-108` — Abstract PlatformCommand strategy with ProcessResult, runStream, runAndWait, findExecutable, shellExecutable/shellArguments
- `/lib/features/diagnostics/engine/runner/platform_impl/windows.dart:1-127` — Windows: powershell.exe shell, where/Get-Command lookup
- `/lib/features/diagnostics/engine/runner/platform_impl/macos.dart:1-103` — macOS: /bin/zsh shell, which lookup
- `/lib/features/diagnostics/engine/runner/platform_impl/linux.dart:1-103` — Linux: /bin/bash shell, which lookup
- `/lib/features/diagnostics/engine/runner/platform_impl/android.dart:1-128` — Android: restricted filesystem, /system/bin search paths
- `/lib/features/diagnostics/engine/runner/platform_impl/ios.dart:1-61` — iOS: sandbox-aware, returns descriptive errors
- `/lib/features/diagnostics/engine/runner/platform_impl/mock_simulator.dart:1-537` — Mock simulator with staged output for all 32 tests
- `/lib/features/diagnostics/presentation/providers/diagnostic_provider.dart:1-364` — Riverpod providers: platform command, engine, target, enabled tests, port scan config, run lifecycle (DiagnosticRunState, DiagnosticRunNotifier), dashboard stats
- `/lib/features/diagnostics/presentation/screens/diagnostic_main_screen.dart:1-650` — Main screen: wide/narrow responsive layouts, sidebar (target input, layer checkboxes, port scan config, summary), content (group panels), target analysis
- `/lib/features/diagnostics/presentation/widgets/target_input_panel.dart:1-303` — Target input with debounced auto-enable G4/G5, run/cancel buttons
- `/lib/features/diagnostics/presentation/widgets/port_scan_config.dart:1-155` — Port scan checkbox + custom range inputs
- `/lib/features/diagnostics/presentation/widgets/test_group_panel.dart:1-210` — ExpansionTile per group with counts, progress bars, test items
- `/lib/features/diagnostics/presentation/widgets/test_result_item.dart:1-978` — Result item with pulsing animation, expandable details dialog, context menu, raw output
- `/lib/features/diagnostics/presentation/widgets/live_progress_panel.dart:1-298` — Bottom status bar: dual progress bars, status labels, layer timings
- `/lib/features/dashboard/presentation/screens/dashboard_screen.dart:1-540` — Dashboard: empty state, run info header, summary cards, per-group breakdown with progress, overall stats
- `/lib/features/dashboard/presentation/widgets/summary_cards.dart:1-152` — 2×2 summary card grid (pass/warning/fail/skipped counts)
- `/lib/features/config/presentation/screens/config_screen.dart:1-364` — Config: 5-tab test configuration with select/deselect all, switch list tiles, test descriptions
- `/lib/features/report/presentation/providers/report_provider.dart:1-166` — HTML report generator with inline CSS, group sections, badges, metrics tables
- `/lib/features/report/presentation/screens/report_preview_screen.dart:1-156` — Placeholder report preview (Phase 9 planned)
- `/lib/features/settings/presentation/screens/settings_screen.dart:1-415` — Settings: language toggle (en/zh), SMTP placeholder, about section with version/features

### CHANGES
None.

### RISKS
None observed.

### BLOCKERS
None.

All directories explored. Here is the complete report.

---

### SUMMARY
Recursively explored all five feature directories under `lib/features/` in the NetDiagnostic Flutter project. The codebase follows a clean-architecture pattern with `domain`, `engine`, and `presentation` layers. I found 37 files across 5 feature modules: diagnostics (22 files — the largest), dashboard (3 files), config (2 files), report (3 files), and settings (2 files). Several provider directories exist but are empty (dashboard/providers, config/providers, report/widgets, settings/providers).

### EVIDENCE

**Directory 1: diagnostics** (22 files)
- `lib/features/diagnostics/domain/entities/diagnostic_result.dart` — domain entity for a diagnostic result
- `lib/features/diagnostics/domain/entities/result_property.dart` — domain entity for a result property key/value
- `lib/features/diagnostics/domain/enums/test_status.dart` — enum for test pass/fail/skip states
- `lib/features/diagnostics/engine/diagnostic_engine.dart` — orchestrator/engine that coordinates diagnostic tests
- `lib/features/diagnostics/engine/g1_base.dart` — G1 test group: base abstract class
- `lib/features/diagnostics/engine/g1_linux.dart` — G1 test group: Linux implementation

- `lib/features/diagnostics/engine/g2_base.dart` — G2 test group: base abstract class
- `lib/features/diagnostics/engine/g2_linux.dart` — G2 test group: Linux implementation

- `lib/features/diagnostics/engine/g2_windows.dart` — G2 test group: Windows implementation
- `lib/features/diagnostics/engine/g3_base.dart` — G3 test group: base abstract class

### CHANGES
None.

### RISKS
- Four provider/widget directories are empty (`dashboard/presentation/providers`, `config/presentation/providers`, `report/presentation/widgets`, `settings/presentation/providers`), which may indicate planned but unimplemented features or stale directory scaffolding. Mitigation: confirm with the team whether these are intended future work or should be cleaned up.
- The diagnostics module is substantially heavier than the other four features; if the app is meant for production, the thin dashboard/config/report/settings modules may be incomplete. Mitigation: cross-reference against a feature-completeness checklist.

### BLOCKERS
None.

workers.result_summary:
I have all the data. Here is the complete report.

---

### SUMMARY
I recursively explored the entire `/lib/core/` directory and read every file under `core/constants/`, `core/theme/`, `core/router/`, `core/services/`, and the root `logger.dart`. The `core/widgets/` directory is empty — no widget files exist there. The design system is defined across 8 files: a dark theme with Phosphor icon registry, a file-based logger, test ID/group enums, default test targets, a GoRouter with 6 routes, a permission service, and a native FFI diagnostic service wrapper. All file contents were retrieved in full.

### EVIDENCE
- `lib/core/` directory listing: 5 subdirectories (`constants`, `router`, `theme`, `widgets`, `services`) + 1 file (`logger.dart`)
- `lib/core/constants/`: 2 files — `test_ids.dart`, `test_targets.dart`
- `lib/core/theme/`: 2 files — `app_theme.dart`, `app_icons.dart`
- `lib/core/widgets/`: **empty** (no files)
- `lib/core/router/`: 1 file — `app_router.dart`
- `lib/core/services/`: 2 files — `permission_service.dart`, `native_diagnostic_service.dart`
- `lib/core/logger.dart:1-50` — `AppLogger` class: file-based sequential logger writing to `%TEMP%/NetDiagnostic/debug.log` (Windows) or `/tmp/NetDiagnostic/debug.log` (Linux/macOS). Methods: `info()`, `event()`, `error()`, `warn()`. Uses an async queue with `_enqueue`/`_flush`.
- `lib/core/constants/test_ids.dart:1-92` — `TestGroup` enum (g1–g5 with labels and timeouts), `TestId` enum (28 total tests across 5 groups, each with a `labelKey` for localization).
- `lib/core/constants/test_targets.dart:1-30` — `TestTargets` class with static const defaults: `connectivityHost` = `223.5.5.5`, `trustedDns` = `223.5.5.5`, `dnsTestDomains` = `['aliyun.com','qq.com','baidu.com']`, `speedTestUrl` = `https://www.aliyun.com`, `speedTestMinBytes` = 100KB.
- `lib/core/theme/app_theme.dart:1-44` — `AppTheme` class: dark color palette (`bgDark=#1E1E2E`, `bgSidebar=#252538`, `bgCard=#16213E`, `accent=#E94560`, `accentBlue=#0078D4`, plus status colors pass/warn/fail/skip/cyan), `fontFamily='JetBrainsMono'`, `darkTheme` getter returning a `ThemeData` with `ColorScheme.dark`, `AppBarTheme`, and `CardThemeData` (rounded 12px, no elevation).
- `lib/core/theme/app_icons.dart:1-82` — `AppIcon` enum using Phosphor Icons with fill + regular variants. Covers status icons, navigation, test groups, actions, platforms, and misc. Includes `TestStatusIcon` extension mapping `TestStatus` enum to `AppIcon`.
- `lib/core/router/app_router.dart:1-44` — `appRouterProvider` (Riverpod `Provider<GoRouter>`) with `initialLocation: '/diagnostics'` and 6 `GoRoute` entries: `/dashboard`, `/diagnostics`, `/config`, `/report`, `/settings`, `/simulator`. Each maps to a corresponding `Screen` widget from `features/`.
- `lib/core/services/permission_service.dart:1-117` — `PermissionService` singleton checking `Permission.locationWhenInUse` for WiFi tests on Android, with `checkPermissions()`, `canRunDiagnostic()`, and `applyPermissionResult()` to degrade results when permissions are limited.
- `lib/core/services/native_diagnostic_service.dart:1-130` — `NativeDiagnosticService` singleton wrapping C++ FFI (`nd_ffi_bindings.dart`). Maps Dart `TestId` to C `NdDiagnosticId` via `_toNativeId()`. G5 HTTP tests (URL parsing, curl, headers, SSL, redirect, compression, timing) return -1 = Dart-only. `runDiagnostic()` returns `DiagnosticResult?` with fallback to Dart on error or `ND_ERR_NOT_SUPPORTED`.

### CHANGES
None.

### RISKS
- `core/widgets/` is empty — UI widget components do not live under `lib/core/widgets/` as expected. Actual reusable widgets may reside in `features/*/presentation/widgets/` or similar feature-level directories, which were not explored per the scope of this task. This could be a project organization mismatch between what the task expected and the actual codebase layout.
- The `speedTestUrl` points to `https://www.aliyun.com` — a homepage download for speed estimation is explicitly noted in the source as unreliable ("NOT a calibrated speed-test payload"), flagged as WARNING below 100KB. This is a known design concession for mainland China accessibility but could produce misleading speed results.
- The `TestId` enum has more entries in `test_ids.dart` (28) than the native mapping in `native_diagnostic_service.dart` (30 cases, but G5 HTTP tests return -1 = Dart-only, plus G5 URL parsing has no case in the switch). This asymmetry could lead to maintenance drift if new tests are added.

### BLOCKERS
None.
